explanation: Benzyl salicylate is a fragrance compound and UV absorber found naturally in many flowers, classified as one of 26 known fragrance allergens required to be disclosed by name on EU cosmetic labels. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, particularly with repeated leave-on exposure.

Bertholletia excelsa (Brazil nut) seed oil is a rich emollient pressed from Brazil nut kernels, high in oleic, linoleic, and palmitic fatty acids along with vitamin E that nourish and soften skin. It absorbs well for a dense oil, supports barrier repair, and is well-tolerated by dry and mature skin types.

Beta-carotene is a carotenoid antioxidant and provitamin A precursor found in orange and yellow plants, used in skincare as a free-radical scavenger and pigment that imparts a warm tone to formulas. It is well-tolerated and contributes antioxidant protection at the skin surface, though it converts to retinol only minimally through topical application compared to dietary intake.

Beta-glucan is a polysaccharide derived from oat, yeast, or mushroom sources that functions as a potent humectant and immune-modulating skin conditioner, drawing moisture into the skin and activating reparative responses in keratinocytes. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and particularly valued for its ability to calm reactive, sensitized, and barrier-compromised skin.

Beta-sitosterol is a plant sterol found in many vegetable oils and plant extracts, used in skincare as an emollient that integrates into the skin's lipid bilayer to support barrier repair and reduce inflammation. It is well-tolerated and non-comedogenic, structurally similar to cholesterol and effective at reinforcing the skin barrier in dry or compromised skin.

Betaine is a naturally occurring zwitterionic compound derived from sugar beet molasses that functions as a humectant and osmoprotectant, drawing moisture into skin cells and helping them resist environmental stress. It has a mild, soothing quality and is exceptionally well-tolerated — often used in formulas for sensitive and reactive skin for its ability to hydrate without any sting or irritation.

Betaine salicylate is the betaine salt of salicylic acid, providing gentle BHA-type exfoliating and pore-clearing activity at a higher pH than free salicylic acid, which translates to lower irritation potential. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and is commonly used in K-beauty formulas as a mild alternative to traditional salicylic acid exfoliants.

Betula alba (white birch) juice is spring-harvested birch sap used in skincare as a hydrating botanical water rich in amino acids, minerals, and xylitol. It is well-tolerated and used in toners and essences as a gentle, mineral-rich alternative to plain water.

Betula platyphylla japonica (Japanese white birch) juice is spring-harvested birch sap from a Japanese birch species, providing amino acids, minerals, and polyphenol antioxidants that hydrate and protect the skin surface. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and hydrating formulas for its antioxidant and mineral content.

BHA (butylated hydroxyanisole) is a synthetic phenolic antioxidant used at very low concentrations in skincare as a preservative and formula stabilizer to prevent oxidative degradation of oils and fats. It is considered well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use levels, though it is not to be confused with the exfoliant salicylic acid, which is sometimes abbreviated BHA in consumer skincare contexts.

BHT (butylated hydroxytoluene) is a synthetic phenolic antioxidant used at very low concentrations in skincare as a formula preservative that prevents fats and oils from going rancid. It is considered well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations and is widely used in both food and personal care products as a stabilizer.

Bioactive 8 Berry Complex is a proprietary blend of antioxidant-rich berry extracts — the asterisk indicates the ingredients are organic-certified. Typical berry complexes combine polyphenols and anthocyanins from sources such as blueberry, raspberry, and cranberry to deliver broad-spectrum antioxidant protection and free-radical scavenging. The specific formula varies by manufacturer and cannot be fully characterized without the complete ingredient disclosure.

Biosaccharide gum-1 is a fermentation-derived polysaccharide (produced by Leuconostoc bacteria) that functions as a humectant, soothing agent, and prebiotic-like skin conditioner. It is exceptionally well-tolerated, forms a soft hydrating film on the skin surface, and is used in formulas for sensitive and reactive skin for its ability to calm irritation and attract moisture.

Bis-capryloyloxypalmitamido isopropanol is a synthetic fatty acid ester used as an emollient and skin-feel enhancer in moisturizers and serums, providing a smooth, non-greasy finish. It is considered well-tolerated and non-comedogenic at typical use concentrations.

Bis-diglyceryl polyacyladipate-2 is a plant-derived polyglycerol ester used as a lightweight, non-greasy emollient in skincare formulas that provides a soft, velvety skin feel without occlusive heaviness. It is considered well-tolerated and is used in moisturizers and lip products as a comfortable, barrier-friendly alternative to heavier waxes.

Bis-ethylhexyloxyphenol methoxyphenyl triazine (Tinosorb S) is a photostable broad-spectrum UV filter that absorbs both UVA and UVB radiation without degrading in sunlight — a significant advantage over photounstable filters like avobenzone. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and considered one of the most effective and modern chemical UV filters available, though it is currently approved only outside the US.

Bis-PEG-18 methyl ether dimethyl silane is a PEG-modified silicone used as an emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent in water-based and silicone-based formulas, providing a smooth, spreadable texture. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol component.

Bis-PEG/PPG-14/14 dimethicone is a PEG/PPG-modified dimethicone used as an emulsifier in water-in-silicone formulas, enabling the creation of lightweight, fast-absorbing textures that feel drier on skin than traditional emulsions. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

Bisabolol (alpha-bisabolol) is a sesquiterpene alcohol found naturally in German chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) with well-documented anti-inflammatory, skin-soothing, and penetration-enhancing properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated — notably less sensitizing than the chamomile essential oil it is derived from — and is widely used in formulas for sensitive, reactive, and post-procedure skin to reduce redness and irritation.

Bixa orellana (annatto) seed oil is pressed from annatto seeds and is exceptionally rich in tocotrienols (a highly potent form of vitamin E) and carotenoids that provide antioxidant protection and support skin barrier function. It is well-tolerated, has a warm golden color, and is used in nourishing and protective formulas for dry and mature skin.

Blueberry extract is derived from Vaccinium corymbosum or related species and is rich in anthocyanins and polyphenol antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and provide mild brightening and anti-aging benefits. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and tone-evening formulas for its broad-spectrum antioxidant activity.

Borago officinalis (borage) seed oil is one of the richest plant sources of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA, ~20–24%), an omega-6 fatty acid that supports barrier repair and reduces inflammation in dry, eczema-prone, and reactive skin. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and is particularly valued for its ability to replenish the skin's deficient fatty acid profile in conditions like eczema and rosacea.

Borago officinalis (borage) seed oil — the asterisk indicates organic certification — is one of the richest plant sources of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that supports barrier repair and reduces inflammation in dry, eczema-prone, and reactive skin. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and is particularly valued in formulas targeting compromised or inflamed barriers.

Boron nitride is a synthetic mineral compound used in cosmetics as a soft-focus texture agent, light diffuser, and slip enhancer, imparting a silky, powder-like skin feel to formulas. The fine hexagonal particles are considered inert and well-tolerated, and they are widely used in primers, setting powders, and mineral makeup for their ability to blur the appearance of pores and fine lines.

Brassica oleracea acephala (kale) leaf extract is a cruciferous vegetable extract rich in glucosinolates, vitamins C and K, and carotenoid antioxidants that help protect skin from oxidative stress and environmental damage. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its antioxidant and mild anti-inflammatory properties.

Brassica oleracea capitata (cabbage) leaf extract is a cruciferous vegetable extract containing glucosinolates, vitamin C, and anti-inflammatory compounds with antioxidant and mild skin-soothing properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective formulas for its ability to neutralize free radicals and support skin comfort.

Brassica oleracea italica (broccoli) extract is rich in sulforaphane, vitamin C, and glucosinolates with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and UV-protective properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and brightening formulas for its ability to activate the skin's own antioxidant defense pathways.

Butyl avocadate is a synthetic ester derived from avocado fatty acids and butanol, used as a lightweight emollient that provides skin conditioning and a smooth, non-greasy finish. It is well-tolerated and has low comedogenic potential, making it suitable for combination and oily skin types alongside its primary use in moisturizing formulas.

Butyl methoxydibenzoylmethane (avobenzone) is the most widely used UVA chemical sunscreen filter globally, providing broad-spectrum protection against long-wave UVA radiation. It is photounstable — breaking down in sunlight within a few hours unless stabilized by other UV filters — and can cause contact and photocontact dermatitis on reactive or sensitive skin, making it one of the more sensitizing chemical sunscreen actives.

Butylene glycol is a short-chain diol used in skincare as a solvent, humectant, and penetration enhancer, helping to draw moisture to the skin surface and improve the absorption of other ingredients. It is well-tolerated by most skin types and is considered a gentler alternative to propylene glycol, with a lower sensitization profile.
